settings.title: What We Are Doing While You Dance
settings.excerpt: The best compliment we get is that nobody noticed us working, which is rather the point.
settings.body:
Couples often ask what a planner really does on the day itself. The honest answer is that if we are doing it well, you never see most of it.

While you are getting ready, we are chasing the late florist. During dinner, we are quietly fixing the seating mix up nobody else clocked. When a button pops, our assistant Priya has a sewing kit in her bag before you finish the sentence.

The invisible to-do list

Behind the calm there is a constant, quiet stream of small problems being solved before they reach you. Most of them you will never hear about, which is the point. A typical day includes things like these.

  • Greeting and directing vendors as they arrive, so nobody asks you.
  • Keeping the timeline moving without it ever feeling rushed.
  • Handling the small emergencies, from a late delivery to a popped button.

Our job is to absorb the hundred small fires so you only ever feel the warmth of the day. The invisible work is the work.

Why it matters who holds it

If no one is holding all of this, it lands on the couple or a well-meaning relative, and they spend the day on their phone instead of in it. Someone has to carry the logistics, and it should not be you.

The best compliment we get is that nobody noticed us working, which is rather the point. You get to be guests at your own wedding, and we will quietly handle everything that would otherwise pull you away from it.
